summary: README-only documentation cleanup that reformatted the project overview, fixed or reorganized links, added badge-style navigation for Discord and the important warning, converted Built DOL metadata into a table, and simplified the contributions section by linking to CONTRIBUTING.md near the top. No source, matching, or decompilation logic changed.

key_files.reason: Only changed file; received 36 additions and 20 deletions updating README layout, badges, contribution link placement, build instructions formatting, and reference links.

what_changed: Changed the README title line from using a `Discord Badge` reference to a renamed `Badge Discord` reference.
Moved the contribution guidelines link from a plain inline link and later contributions section into a prominent centered kbd-style link near the top, backed by `[Guidelines]: CONTRIBUTING.md`.

Added centered layout markup with `
` and several `
` spacers to adjust visual presentation.
Replaced the `## ⚠ Important` heading with a shields.io `Important` badge image reference.
Converted Built DOL details for version `1.02`, file `main.dol`, and SHA `08e0bf20134dfcb260699671004527b2d6bb1a45` from plain labeled lines into a Markdown table.
Retained the existing warning that the built DOL can be shifted and that code can be added or removed for modding and research.
Adjusted Building section spacing and punctuation, including `Run the **Make** command:`.
Removed the separate `## Contributions` section that said pull requests are welcome, leaving contribution guidance represented by the top-level guidelines link.
Reorganized link reference definitions, including DevKitPro, Discord, Python3, GCC, GC_WII_COMPILERS, Guidelines, Badge Important, and Badge Discord.
smart_moves: Kept contribution guidance visible by moving the CONTRIBUTING.md link near the top instead of only at the bottom of the README.
Preserved critical build identity information, especially the main.dol SHA, while changing its presentation into a compact table.
Used reference-style Markdown links for repeated or visually noisy URLs, making the README body easier to scan.
Grouped badge reference definitions under a dedicated badge comment block, which makes future badge edits easier to find.
decomp_lessons: Documentation-only PRs can still matter for contributor onboarding: prominent CONTRIBUTING.md, compiler download, and build command links reduce setup friction for future decomp contributors.
When changing README presentation, preserve exact technical values such as target version, output file name, and expected SHA because those are part of the build/matching contract.
For repository-facing docs, prefer centralized reference links for external setup dependencies such as DevKitPro, Python3, GCC, and GC_WII_COMPILERS so future URL fixes are localized.
This PR provides no evidence about C matching tactics, symbol naming, struct layout, or assembly work.

naming_conventions.convention: README reference labels for visual badge assets use `Badge <Name>` form.
Contribution documentation is referenced via `[Guidelines]: CONTRIBUTING.md` rather than repeating the full GitHub URL.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: None evidenced; the PR only modifies README.md and contains no code, assembly, linker, or matching changes.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.

The PR body asked maintainers to check the preview and listed repository suggestions such as adding tags, choosing a license, and disabling unused GitHub widgets or tabs, but there is no evidence these suggestions were acted on in this PR.
